opp.name: Signet Jewelers_CLM Merlin, Merlin Intake, Spend Analytics
opp.next_step: Dry-run demo focused on Intake + CLM for Erin, Megan, and DeVonn before General Counsel review: 6/24/2026 Signet to share workflow diagrams and sample NDA template for a tailored demo. Completed Zycus to send pricing/budgetary questionnaire. sent 6/23/2026 Zycus to provide budgetary proposal within 1–2 weeks. Align demo around SAP, Workday, and Adobe Sign integrations. Prepare follow-up discussion on Spend Analytics, Catalog, and eSourcing as next-phase opportunities.
opp.ais_why:
• Signet's core problems are 'manual workflows, lack of visibility, and no linkage between contract data and spend' ‚Äî the driver is CLM and spend analytics rather than AI.
• Merlin and Merlin Intake are scoped and a dry-run demo focused on 'Intake + CLM' is planned, showing openness to Zycus AI-enabled modules.
• Agentic-platform-discussed is not set; interest is real but centered on fixing CLM pain, so appetite is curious-not-hungry.

sources: Clari email 'Zycus Questions for budgetary estimate' 23 Jun 2026
metrics.narrative: The operational pain is clearly articulated but not yet quantified. On the 18 Jun discovery call the buyer described a sunsetting incumbent CLM, manual routing, lack of cross-business visibility, and heavy reliance on third-party supplier paper (~80% of contracts per the 24 Jun workshop takeaways), all driving legal workload. No cycle-time, contract-volume, compliance-incident, or resource-hour figures have been captured to anchor ROI. Zycus has sent a sizing questionnaire (23 Jun email: annual contract volume, # power/business users, # historical contracts, intake requestors, annual spend) which will produce the inputs, but the value case is still qualitative. Quantifying it is a ranked move to harden the budgetary proposal against the Do Nothing baseline.

sources: Clari email received from Erin Shehadi 26 Jun 2026
Clari email 'Follow-Up: MNDA Template & Workflow' from Megan Snyder 18 Jun 2026
champion.narrative: Erin Shehadi (Sr. Manager, Procurement Technology & Enablement) is the developing champion: she owns the proposal conversation, drives scheduling, and on 26 Jun proactively offered to push the recording up to her VP and General Counsel ('I also want to send it to my VP and our General Counsel') — clear internal-selling behaviour. Megan Snyder is an active co-champion who supplied the MNDA template and workflow and pressed detailed CLM requirements in the workshop. What has developed: ownership of next steps and a stated intent to escalate. What is still missing: confirmed access to and a warm introduction to the economic buyer (VP) and legal (GC), and an explicit commitment to advocate in those rooms. The champion is engaged and warm, not yet senior or power-connected.

competition.narrative: No named external software competitor has surfaced in any source. The primary alternative is Do Nothing / staying on the sunsetting incumbent CLM and manual processes, which is weakening as a stay-put option because the incumbent is being retired (a forcing function in our favour). The 24 Jun workshop note that Signet plans a 'formal CLM-focused RFP' signals other vendors may be invited, so a competitive field could open up. Our wedge is the combined Merlin Teams intake agent plus CLM (centralized intake, automated routing, confidentiality, Instareview AI for third-party paper) presented as one platform, plus the prior Zycus relationship with senior procurement (Bancroft, Meyers). How we win: land the budgetary proposal and legal-friendly story before the RFP formalizes, so we shape the criteria.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific and owner-attributed. The incumbent CLM is sunsetting, forcing a replacement; intake is manual with no single front door, redline routing to legal/security/risk/commercial owners is uncontrolled, playbooks exist outside the CLM and are not integrated with the clause library (objection raised by Megan Snyder, 24 Jun), and ~80% of contracts run on third-party supplier paper that legal must manually review. Confidentiality and self-service intake via Teams are explicitly required. The pain owners are the Procurement Technology & Enablement team (Erin Shehadi, Megan Snyder, DeVonn Nixon) and, downstream, Legal/General Counsel who carry the redline workload. Inferred but not yet quantified: hours and cycle-time lost to manual routing.

sources: Clari email 'Follow-Up: MNDA Template & Workflow' 18 Jun 2026
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are emerging. An MNDA is in motion: Signet (Megan Snyder) shared its MNDA template and workflow on 18 Jun, and an NDA template exchange is part of the demo prep. General Counsel review is the explicit legal gate before any agreement. No MSA/SOW, procurement channel, or InfoSec review has surfaced yet, and the incumbent CLM's sunset date (referenced as 'September 2027' in workshop notes, the buyer's replacement deadline) is the practical forcing function. Pricing paper begins once the budgetary proposal lands. Visibility into the full paper process will come once the VP/GC are engaged and the formal RFP scope is set.

sources: Clari email received from Erin Shehadi 26 Jun 2026
Intro call 21 Oct 2025 (Brian Bancroft, Rachelle Meyers referenced)
economic_buyer.narrative: The budget approver has not been engaged. The working team (Erin Shehadi, Megan Snyder, DeVonn Nixon) sits at Manager / Sr. Manager level in Procurement Technology & Enablement and is not the economic buyer for a $350k decision. The strongest live signal is Erin's 26 Jun email: 'I also want to send it to my VP and our General Counsel' — naming, but not yet introducing, the VP (budget) and General Counsel (legal sign-off). Account history references senior procurement leaders Brian Bancroft (SVP Procurement) and Rachelle Meyers from the 2025 relationship, but neither is confirmed on this opportunity. With an August internal-decision target and September budget season, securing the VP's name, an introduction, and budget-envelope validation is the single highest-leverage gap to close.

decision_process.narrative: The process is becoming visible. The buyer is running a structured evaluation: discovery (18 Jun) → dry-run intake/CLM demo for the working team (24 Jun) → internal review of the recording → presentation to the relevant stakeholder for legal sign-off → VP/budget review, targeting an internal decision by August ahead of the September budget cycle. The 24 Jun workshop takeaway also notes Signet intends to 'proceed with a formal CLM-focused RFP', so an RFP gate may sit between now and selection. Legal sign-off is a named formal gate (General Counsel review). The approval chain above the working team (VP, GC) is identified by role but not yet engaged. A mutual close/decision plan with dates would de-risk the August target.

sources: CLM Workshop 24 Jun 2026 key takeaways and demo Q&A
decision_criteria.narrative: Evaluation criteria are concrete and documented from the 18 Jun discovery and 24 Jun workshop. Confirmed criteria: replace the sunsetting CLM (top priority); Microsoft Teams-exposed intake agent (Merlin) for self-service NDA/amendment requests with policy routing and auto-trigger workflows; 'legal-friendly' CLM with serial and parallel redline review, clause/template libraries and Instareview AI for third-party paper (~80% of contracts); Microsoft Word in-Word redlining plugin; integrations to SAP (vendor master, authority limits), Workday (approval hierarchy) and Adobe Sign / e-signature; ad-hoc approver/reviewer addition for VP/CFO/CLO delegation-of-authority exceptions; and reporting/aging metrics per review queue. These were demoed live against Signet's own workflow and NDA template.

items.detail: Economic Buyer (SVP/VP Procurement) not yet engaged; budgetary proposal requested for August internal decision ahead of September budget cycle, but budget approver has not been introduced or validated the $350k envelope. If proposal does not align with EB priorities or budget, deal will defer to next fiscal year.

General Counsel not yet engaged; dry-run demo 24 Jun framed as preparation 'before General Counsel review,' but GC has not seen the solution or validated fit. Legal sign-off is a formal gate, and GC engagement before the August decision is critical to prevent approval-cycle delay.

Quantified business case missing: operational pain is clear (manual routing, no visibility, integration gaps) but not quantified (cycle-time, compliance incidents, resource hours wasted). Without hard ROI anchoring the budgetary proposal, the urgency vs. Do Nothing is weak and the August decision may defer.

Legal sponsor (General Counsel or senior legal counsel) not yet engaged; dry-run demo 24 Jun framed as preparation 'before General Counsel review,' but the legal function has not seen the solution or validated fit. Legal sign-off is a formal gate, and without engagement before the August decision, approval-cycle delay is likely.

Compressed timeline: 5 weeks from now to August internal decision, but critical validation gates (EB engagement, quantified ROI, legal sponsor validation) are not yet complete. If these gates are not cleared by mid-July, the deal will miss the August decision window and defer to next fiscal year.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-06-18, 12 days in Qualified as of today (2026-06-30). Days to close: 171 (close 2026-12-18). Buyer's stated internal decision target is August 2026 with September budget season, so the buyer's clock (~6-9 weeks) is tighter than the SF close date. Last activity 2026-06-29 (1 day ago); buyer email received 2026-06-26 (4 days ago). Three buyer touchpoints in 8 months, two in the last 12 days. Pace is healthy for the stage.
evidence: Discovery call 18 Jun 2026: 'target internal decision by August; budget season in September for next fiscal year' (speaker_id 3, exec summary).
CLM Workshop 24 Jun 2026 delivered as the dry-run before General Counsel review (manifest touchpoint 3).
Email received from Erin Shehadi 26 Jun 2026: 'I also want to send it to my VP and our General Counsel' (Clari inbound, Task 26 Jun).
Shared Space with recording created and shared to Erin, Megan, DeVonn 29 Jun 2026 (Task 29 Jun).
north_star_verdict.headline: Live, fast-moving CLM replacement: dry-run demo delivered 24 Jun, recording shared 29 Jun, buyer asking to loop in VP and General Counsel; close date (18 Dec) has ample runway but buyer's own internal decision target is August.

flags: Economic Buyer not engaged: no CFO, CPO, or SVP-level participation on any call; account contacts include SVP Procurement (Brian Bancroft) and VP Procurement Operations (Ashley Winkleman), but neither has joined. Ask Erin to introduce the budget approver and schedule a value discussion before the budgetary proposal is finalized (by early Jul) to prevent August decision from stalling at approval.

Multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder and other decision influencers: dry-run demo (24 Jun) was framed as preparation 'before General Counsel review,' but GC has not been engaged yet. The 18 Jun call named security, risk, and commercial owners as stakeholders in the redline-routing workflow, but none have participated. Before the August decision, ensure GC and other approval-gate owners have seen the solution and validated fit to reduce approval-cycle risk.
best_practice_check.summary: Live, well-engaged CLM replacement with a clear compelling event (incumbent sunset) and an active champion; the win levers are getting power and legal into the room and converting demonstrated fit into a quantified, budget-aligned proposal before the August decision.

competitive_position.summary: No named software rival in evidence; the live competition is Do Nothing / the incumbent CLM, which is weakening because that incumbent is sunsetting (a forcing function for us). A 'formal CLM-focused RFP' the buyer plans (24 Jun) could introduce vendors later, so the priority is to shape criteria before the RFP formalizes. Strongest current threat: timing/budget slippage to next fiscal year, not a competitor.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Manual Process

competitors.how_we_win: Quantify cost of continuing manually (cycle-time, compliance incidents, resource hours wasted) and anchor budgetary proposal to compelling ROI that makes deferral more expensive than proceeding. Secure EB sponsorship to validate budget envelope and urgency.

deltas.to: Erin Shehadi (Director CLM) - developing
deltas.subject: champion

General Counsel not yet engaged; dry-run demo 24 Jun framed as preparation 'before General Counsel review,' but GC has not seen the solution or validated fit. Legal sign-off is a formal gate, and GC engagement before the August decision is critical to prevent approval-cycle delay.

Economic Buyer not engaged: no CFO, CPO, or SVP-level participation on any call; account contacts include SVP Procurement (Brian Bancroft) and VP Procurement Operations (Ashley Winkleman), but neither has joined. Ask Erin to introduce the budget approver and schedule a value discussion before the budgetary proposal is finalized (by early Jul) to prevent August decision from stalling at approval.

Quantified business case missing: buyer articulated operational pain (manual routing, no visibility, integration gaps) but has not quantified cycle-time, compliance incidents, or resource hours wasted. Co-develop a value framework tying contract throughput, error rates, and hours saved to dollar savings, and anchor the budgetary proposal in hard ROI to make deferral more expensive than proceeding.

Multi-thread to the relevant stakeholder and other decision influencers: dry-run demo (24 Jun) was framed as preparation 'before General Counsel review,' but GC has not been engaged yet. The 18 Jun call named security, risk, and commercial owners as stakeholders in the redline-routing workflow, but none have participated. Before the August decision, ensure GC and other approval-gate owners have seen the solution and validated fit to reduce approval-cycle risk.
